prompt.prompt_text:
You are analyzing a television commercial for corpus-linguistic and multimodal discourse analysis.

You are given sampled frames from a video clip. The frames are in chronological order.
Treat the frames as a temporal sequence from one commercial, not as independent images.

Describe the visual content of the commercial as it unfolds over time.
Describe only what is visually supported by the frames.

Do not guess dialogue, slogans, music, voice-over, or other audio content.
If something is unclear or not visible in the sampled frames, say so.

output.response_text:
The commercial opens on an almost completely dark frame. No clear objects, people, setting, or text are visually discernible at the start.

By the final sampled frame, the image is in black and white and shows an interior domestic setting, likely a kitchen or laundry area. A woman stands near the center-right of the frame, facing toward the camera. She is wearing a dress or blouse-and-skirt outfit and appears to be holding a light-colored cloth or garment in both hands. Behind and around her are household fixtures: a counter or sink area to the left, cabinetry below, a curtained window with decorative drapery in the background, and a large upright appliance on the right that resembles a refrigerator. There is also a wall-mounted decorative object or shelf behind her on the right. The woman’s posture is upright and presentational, as if demonstrating or displaying the cloth, but no specific action beyond holding it is visible in the sampled frame.

No readable on-screen text is visible in these frames, and no audio information can be inferred from the images.
